title: Payroll Cost Trajectory 2022-2026
description: Monthly payroll actuals (salary + benefits disbursements) extracted from SP_Analysis daily cash forecast files. All amounts in dollars, negative = outflow. For each month, actuals are sourced from the earliest file where the month is fully closed (act/forecast ratio >= 0.85). Files typically carry 5-8 months of history.

methodology.format_A_files: Sep 2022, Dec 2022 — monthly totals in blank-date rows preceding next month label
methodology.format_B_files: Apr 2023 onwards — monthly totals in abbreviated month-name rows (e.g., 'Feb', 'Oct')
methodology.full_month_criterion: Actuals considered full-month when act/forecast ratio >= 0.85
methodology.partial_months_flagged: Current-month actuals (file date mid-month) are flagged as partial
methodology.supplementary_files: Aug 19, Sep 24, Oct 15, Nov 12, Dec 10 2024 files used to fill 2024 gaps

canonical_monthly_series.note: Sep 22 BOD only has partial Sep actuals through Sep 19 ($31.97M); full Sep from Dec 22 BOD = $48.38M
Dec 22 BOD file only has Dec actuals through Dec 7 ($13.68M); full Dec from Apr 23 BOD = $39.50M
March 2023 includes biweekly cycle; large run on Mar 31
Apr 23 BOD has partial Apr actuals $18.16M through Apr 25; full Apr from Aug 23 BOD = $32.84M
Aug 23 BOD has partial Aug actuals $25.72M through Aug 22; full Aug from Jan 24 BOD = $27.08M
Jan 24 BOD has partial Jan actuals $22.18M through Jan 23; no later file in set covers full Jan 2024
Jul 24 BOD file has partial Jul actuals $15.64M through Jul 23; full Jul from Aug 19 2024 file = $29.44M
Nov 12 BOD has partial Nov actuals $7.23M through Nov 12; no later file in target set covers full Nov 2024
Forecast-actual gap likely includes year-end severance or bonus accruals outside this sheet
Jan 25 BOD has partial Jan actuals $12.21M through Jan 21; full Jan from May 25 BOD = $24.15M
May 25 BOD has partial May actuals $10.66M through May 20; full May from Oct 25 BOD = $20.94M
Oct 25 BOD has partial Oct actuals $10.58M through Oct 21; full Oct from Dec 30 2025 file = $19.95M
Dec 30 file has 30 of 31 days; large fcst-actual gap ($7M) suggests severance/bonuses not loaded or paid in different month
Apr 26 BOD has partial Apr actuals $9.50M through Apr 21; full Apr from May 8 2026 file = $19.54M
May 8 file has actuals only through May 8 = $5.17M; full month forecast = $19.23M

yearly_averages.note: 2024 and 2025 averages exclude months where no full-month actual is available (Jan 2024, Nov 2024, Jan 2025)

key_question_1_sep2022_vs_may2026.question: Total monthly payroll Sep 2022 vs May 2026

sep_2022.note: Sep 22 BOD file (dated Sep 19) shows only partial Sep actuals of $31.97M through Sep 19. Full Sep 2022 = $48.38M from Dec 22 BOD.

apr_2026_last_full_month.note: Apr 21 BOD file has partial Apr actuals of $9.50M through Apr 21. Full Apr 2026 = $19.54M from May 8 file. May 2026 is only partial ($5.17M through May 8).

implied_headcount_at_key_salary_assumpti.assumption_note: Monthly cost per employee = annual total compensation / 12. Total comp = salary + ~25-30% benefits.

key_question_2_pre_post_payroll_change.question: Quantify payroll change: Apr 21, 2026 (pre) vs May 8, 2026 (post)
key_question_2_pre_post_payroll_change.context: Apr 21, 2026 BOD file is labeled 'pre payroll change'. May 8, 2026 file reflects post-change.

apr_26_BOD_last_full_month_actual.source: Apr 21 BOD file shows partial Apr actuals $9.50M through Apr 21

may_08_forward_forecast.note: May 2026 actuals only through May 8 = $5.17M; forecast for full May = $19.23M

assessed_change.finding: Payroll schedule (FY26 detail tab) reduced by $3.48M annual (-0.7%) between Apr 21 and May 8 files. Cash flow forward forecast shows $120-150K/month reduction. The headcount change is small in percentage terms but real.
assessed_change.weekly_note: May 2-8 week ($5.17M) is higher than Apr 15-21 week ($2.03M) due to normal biweekly payroll cycle timing: the May 2-8 week captured a major Home Services biweekly run ($4.09M vs $0.78M the prior measured week).

key_question_2_pre_post_payroll_change.payroll_tab_comparison_note: The Apr 21 BOD file has tabs: 'FY27 payroll 4.17', 'FY26 payroll 4.17'. The May 8 BOD file has tabs: 'FY27 payroll 5.8', 'FY26 payroll 5.8'. A direct comparison of these dedicated payroll schedule tabs would give the precise headcount change. The Payroll main tab aggregate data shows only a $120K/month difference in forward forecast.

payroll_schedule_comparison.data_source: FY26 payroll detail tabs: 'FY26 payroll 4.17' (Apr 21 BOD) vs 'FY26 payroll 5.8' (May 8 file)
payroll_schedule_comparison.interpretation: The 'payroll change' represents a headcount reduction effective April 2026 that reduced the annualized FY26 payroll run-rate by $3.48M (-0.7%). Monthly equivalent reduction: ~$290K/month. April 2026 fiscal period shows the largest single-period change ($1.23M). Note: The payroll schedule tabs appear to use a different basis than the cash flow actuals (possibly gross payroll vs net disbursements, or may include employers' share of benefits differently). Cash flow forward forecast shows only ~$120K/month difference.

workforce_reduction_trajectory.narrative:
Payroll costs show a clear but non-linear decline with several structural step-downs:
1. Sep 2022 peak (~$48M/mo): Pre-restructuring baseline
2. H2 2022 restructuring: Declined from $48M (Sep) to $28M (Aug) — ~$20M reduction in one quarter
3. 2023 volatility: Monthly costs ranged $26M-$43M, with large biweekly payroll spike months
4. 2024 stability: Costs stabilized around $27-$36M/mo through most of 2024
5. Late 2024 / early 2025 reduction: Dropped sharply to $22-24M/mo range by early 2025
6. Mid-2025 step-down: Further reduction to $19-22M/mo through H2 2025
7. Q4 2025: Stabilized at $18-20M/mo
8. 2026: Jan-Apr holding ~$19M/mo — appears stable post prior reductions
workforce_reduction_trajectory.spike_pattern_note: Biweekly payroll creates a 3-payroll month pattern (~every 6 months). Months like Sep, Mar, Jun frequently show 20-30% higher payroll vs adjacent months. The Sep 2022 spike ($48.4M) also appears to include severance payments.
